title: phoradendron tomentosum subsp. macrophyllum
intro: Phoradendron tomentosum subsp. macrophyllum, also known as the Mexican mistletoe or broad-leaved mistletoe, is a type of evergreen parasitic plant native to Mexico and Central America that grows on the branches of trees, such as oak and pine. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Phoradendron tomentosum subsp. macrophyllum include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe respiratory issues, and provide relief from fever. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
